briefdescription.para: Minimum byte alignment requirement for descriptors.
Minimum word alignment requirement for descriptors.
Maximum number of the frame store.

detaileddescription.para: Maximum # of the frame store

briefdescription.para: Maximum # of the frame store for 64 bit.

description.para: Register sets on TX (Read) and RX (Write) channels are identical
The version register is shared by both channels

sectiondef.header: Start Addresses Register Array for a Channel

description.para: Base offset is set in each channel This set of registers are write only, they can be read when C_ENABLE_VIDPRMTR_READS is 1.
This register reports status of a DMA channel, including run/stop/idle state, errors, and interrupts
These masks are shared by the XAXIVDMA_CR_OFFSET register and the XAXIVDMA_SR_OFFSET register

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and shift for delay and coalesce

description.para: These masks are shared by the XAXIVDMA_CR_OFFSET register and the XAXIVDMA_SR_OFFSET register

briefdescription.para: Masks S2MM IRQ FSize Less/SOF Early Error.
Masks S2MM IRQ LSize Less/EOL Early Error.
Masks S2MM IRQ FSize More/SOF Late Error.
Masks S2MM IRQ LSize More/EOL Late Error.

sectiondef.header: Frame Delay shared by start address registers and BDs

briefdescription.para: Total number of words for one BD.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is called.
indicates the detailed type(s) of the interrupt. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XAXIVDMA_IXR_* values defined in xaxivdma_hw.h
is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback function, and it is passed back to the upper layer when the callback is called.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XAXIVDMA_IXR_* values defined in xaxivdma_hw.h

briefdescription.para: BaseAddress is the physical base address of the device's registers
The maximum number of Frame Stores.
Whether hw build has read channel.
Whether hw build has write channel.
Read Enable for video parameters in direct register mode.
DMA operations synchronized to Frame Sync.
VDMA Transactions are flushed & channel states reset on Frame Sync
Depth of Read Channel Line Buffer FIFO.
Depth of Write Channel Line Buffer FIFO.
MM2S Threshold Register Enable Flag This corresponds to C_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_1 configuration parameter.
MM2S Frame Store Register Enable Flag This corresponds to C_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_5 configuration parameter.
MM2S Delay Counter (Control Reg) Enable Flag This corresponds to C_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_6 configuration parameter.
MM2S Frame Counter (Control Reg) Enable Flag This corresponds to C_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_7 configuration parameter.
S2MM Threshold Register Enable Flag This corresponds to C_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_9 configuration parameter.
S2MM Frame Store Register Enable Flag This corresponds to C_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_13 configuration parameter.
S2MM Delay Counter (Control Reg) Enable Flag This corresponds to C_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_14 configuration parameter.
S2MM Frame Counter (Control Reg) Enable Flag This corresponds to C_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_15 configuration parameter.
Enable all Debug features This corresponds to C_ENABLE_DEBUG_ALL configuration parameter.
Whether hardware has Vertical Flip enabled(c_enable_vert_flip)
Bits[11:0] Interrupt-id Bits[15:12] trigger type and level flags.
Bit[0] Interrupt parent type Bit[64/32:1] Parent base address.
Start Addresses of Frame Store Buffers.
Number of frames to work on.
Whether hw build has read channel.
Whether hw build has write channel.
Read Enable for video parameters in direct register mode.
DMA operations synchronized to Frame Sync.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA engine to work on
is the direction for the channel to get

simplesect.para: The pointer to the channel. Upon error, return NULL.
Since this function is internally used, we assume Direction is valid

briefdescription.para: Initialize the driver with hardware configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the pointer to the hardware configuration structure
is the virtual address map for the device

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything goes fine
XST_FAILURE if reset the hardware failed, need system reset to recover

simplesect.para: If channel fails reset, then it will be set as invalid

briefdescription.para: This function resets one DMA channel.

detaileddescription.para: The registers will be default values after the reset

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: Due to undeterminism of system delays, check the reset status through XAxiVdma_ResetNotDone(). If direction is invalid, do nothing.

briefdescription.para: This function checks one DMA channel for reset completion.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

listitem.para: 0 if reset is done
1 if reset is ongoing

simplesect.para: We do not check for channel validity, because channel is marked as invalid before reset is done

briefdescription.para: Check whether a DMA channel is busy.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

listitem.para: Non-zero if the channel is busy
Zero if the channel is idle

briefdescription.para: Get the current frame that hardware is working on.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: The current frame that the hardware is working on
If returned frame number is out of range, then the channel is invalid

briefdescription.para: Get the version of the hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on

briefdescription.para: Get the status of a channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: An invalid return value indicates that channel is invalid

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the value to set threshold
is the DMA channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE otherwise
XST_NO_FEATURE if access to Threshold register is disabled

briefdescription.para: Configure Frame Sync Source and valid only when C_USE_FSYNC is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the value to set the source of Frame Sync
is the DMA channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE if C_USE_FSYNC is disabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the value to set the source of Gen Lock
is the DMA channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE if the channel is in GenLock Master Mode. if C_INCLUDE_INTERNAL_GENLOCK is disabled.

briefdescription.para: Start parking mode on a certain frame.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the frame to park on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything is fine
XST_INVALID_PARAM if . channel is invalid . FrameIndex is invalid . Direction is invalid

briefdescription.para: Exit parking mode, the channel will return to circular buffer mode.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: If channel is invalid, then do nothing

briefdescription.para: Start frame count enable on one channel.

detaileddescription.para: This is needed to start limiting the number of frames to transfer so that software can check the data etc after hardware stops transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u32 BdAddrPhys, u32 BdAddrVirt, int NumBds, u16 Direction)
memberdef.name: XAxiVdma_SetBdAddrs

briefdescription.para: Set BD addresses to be different.

detaileddescription.para: In some systems, it is convenient to put BDs into a certain region of the memory. This function enables that.

InstancePtr
is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
BdAddrPhys
is the physical starting address for BDs
BdAddrVirt
is the Virtual starting address for BDs. For systems that do not use MMU, then virtual address is the same as physical address
NumBds
is the number of BDs to setup with. This is required to be the same as the number of frame stores for that channel
Direction
is the channel direction
XST_SUCCESS for a successful setup
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVALID_PARAM if parameters not valid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid
We assume that the memory region starting from BdAddrPhys and BdAddrVirt are large enough to hold all the BDs.
Write corresponds to send data from device to memory

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the pointer to the setup structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for a successful submission
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if parameters in config structure not valid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid

detaileddescription.para: Read corresponds to send data from memory to device

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the pointer to the setup structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for a successful submission
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if parameters in config structure not valid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid

briefdescription.para: Configure one DMA channel using the configuration structure.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on
is the pointer to the setup structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if buffer address not valid, for example, unaligned address with no DRE built in the hardware, or Direction invalid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid

briefdescription.para: Configure buffer addresses for one DMA channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on
is the set of addresses for the transfers

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if buffer address not valid, for example, unaligned address with no DRE built in the hardware, or Direction invalid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if channel started successfully
XST_FAILURE otherwise
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid
XST_INVALID_PARAM if Direction invalid

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on

simplesect.para: If channel is invalid, then do nothing on that channel

briefdescription.para: Dump registers of one DMA channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on

simplesect.para: If channel is invalid, then do nothing on that channel

briefdescription.para: Set the frame counter and delay counter for both channels.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the pointer to the configuration structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setup finishes successfully
XST_INVALID_PARAM if the configuration structure has invalid values
Others if setting channel frame counter fails

simplesect.para: If channel is invalid, then do nothing on that channel

briefdescription.para: Get the frame counter and delay counter for both channels.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the configuration structure to contain return values

simplesect.para: If returned frame counter value is 0, then the channel is not valid

briefdescription.para: Set the number of frame store buffers to use.

parameterdescription.para: is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the number of frame store buffers to use.
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if operation is successful
XST_FAILURE if operation fails.
XST_NO_FEATURE if access to FrameStore register is disabled

briefdescription.para: Get the number of frame store buffers to use.

parameterdescription.para: is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the number of frame store buffers to use.
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE
is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para:
- Errors seen on the channel
XST_INVALID_PARAM, when channel pointer is invalid.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND, when the channel is not valid.

parameterdescription.para: is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE
is the mask of error bits to clear

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS, when error bits are cleared.
XST_INVALID_PARAM, when channel pointer is invalid.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND, when the channel is not valid.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID/BaseAddress, or NULL if no match is found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Enable specific interrupts for a channel.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts not specified by the mask will not be affected

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE
is the bit mask for the interrupts to be enabled

simplesect.para: If channel is invalid, then nothing is done

briefdescription.para: Disable specific interrupts for a channel.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts not specified by the mask will not be affected

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the bit mask for the interrupts to be disabled
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE

simplesect.para: If channel is invalid, then nothing is done

briefdescription.para: Get the pending interrupts of a channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE

simplesect.para: The bit mask for the currently pending interrupts

briefdescription.para: Clear the pending interrupts specified by the bit mask.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts not specified by the mask will not be affected

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE
is the bit mask for the interrupts to be cleared

simplesect.para: If channel is invalid, then nothing is done

briefdescription.para: Masks the S2MM error interrupt for the provided error mask value.

parameterdescription.para: is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the mask of error bits for which S2MM error interrupt can be disabled.
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS, when error bits are cleared.
XST_INVALID_PARAM, when channel pointer is invalid.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND, when the channel is not valid.

The register S2MM_DMA_IRQ_MASK is only applicable from IPv6.01a which is added at offset XAXIVDMA_S2MM_DMA_IRQ_MASK_OFFSET. For older versions, this offset location is reserved and so the API does not have any effect.

briefdescription.para: Interrupt handler for the read channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on

simplesect.para: If the channel is invalid, then no interrupt handling

briefdescription.para: Interrupt handler for the write channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on

simplesect.para: If the channel is invalid, then no interrupt handling

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u32 HandlerType, void *CallBackFunc, void *CallBackRef, u16 Direction)
memberdef.name: XAxiVdma_SetCallBack

briefdescription.para: Set call back function and call back reference pointer for one channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the interrupt type that this callback handles
is the call back function pointer
is the call back reference pointer
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything is fine
XST_INVALID_PARAM if the handler type or direction invalid

simplesect.para: This function overwrites the existing interrupt handler and its reference pointer. The function sets the handlers even if the channels are invalid.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: This test perform a reset of the VDMA device and checks the device is coming out of reset or not

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiVdma instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if self-test was successful
XST_FAILURE if the device is not coming out of reset.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a channel of a DMA engine.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes the BD ring for this channel

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA channel to work on

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether reset operation is done.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA channel to work on

listitem.para: 0 if reset is done
1 if reset is still going

briefdescription.para: This function resets one DMA channel.

detaileddescription.para: The registers will be default values after the reset

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA channel to work on

briefdescription.para: Check whether a DMA channel is busy.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

listitem.para: non zero if the channel is busy
0 is the channel is idle

briefdescription.para: Get the current status of a channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

briefdescription.para: Set the channel to run in parking mode.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything is fine
XST_FAILURE if hardware is not running

briefdescription.para: Set the channel to run in circular mode, exiting parking mode.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

briefdescription.para: Set the channel to run in frame count enable mode.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

briefdescription.para: Setup BD addresses to a different memory region.

detaileddescription.para: In some systems, it is convenient to put BDs into a certain region of the memory. This function enables that.

Channel
is the pointer to the channel to work on
BdAddrPhys
is the physical starting address for BDs
BdAddrVirt
is the Virtual starting address for BDs. For systems that do not use MMU, then virtual address is the same as physical address
XST_SUCCESS for a successful setup
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
We assume that the memory region starting from BdAddrPhys is large enough to hold all the BDs.
This function setup the DMA engine and start the engine to do the transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on
is the pointer to the setup structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for a successful submission
XST_FAILURE if channel has not being initialized
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if parameters in config structure not valid

briefdescription.para: Configure one DMA channel using the configuration structure.

detaileddescription.para: Setup the control register and BDs, however, BD addresses are not set.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on
is the pointer to the setup structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE if channel has not being initialized
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle
XST_INVALID_PARAM if fields in ChannelCfgPtr is not valid

briefdescription.para: Configure buffer addresses for one DMA channel.

detaileddescription.para: The buffer addresses are physical addresses. Access to 32 Frame Buffer Addresses in direct mode is done through XAxiVdma_ChannelHiFrmAddrEnable/Disable Functions. 0 - Access Bank0 Registers (0x5C - 0x98) 1 - Access Bank1 Registers (0x5C - 0x98)

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on
is the set of addresses for the transfers
is the number of frames to set the address

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE if channel has not being initialized
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if buffer address not valid, for example, unaligned address with no DRE built in the hardware

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE if channel is not initialized
XST_DMA_ERROR if: . The DMA channel fails to stop . The DMA channel fails to start
XST_DEVICE_BUSY is the channel is doing transfers

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

briefdescription.para: Dump registers from one DMA channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

briefdescription.para: Set the frame counter and delay counter for one channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on
is the frame counter value to be set
is the delay counter value to be set

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setup finishes successfully
XST_FAILURE if channel is not initialized
XST_INVALID_PARAM if the configuration structure has invalid values
XST_NO_FEATURE if Frame Counter or Delay Counter is disabled

briefdescription.para: Get the frame counter and delay counter for both channels.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on
is the pointer for the returning frame counter value
is the pointer for the returning delay counter value

simplesect.para: If FrmCnt return as 0, then the channel is not initialized

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts that are not specified by the interrupt mask are not affected.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on
is the interrupt mask for interrupts to be enabled

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts that are not specified by the interrupt mask are not affected.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on
is the interrupt mask for interrupts to be disabled

briefdescription.para: Get pending interrupts of a channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

simplesect.para: The interrupts mask represents pending interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts that are not specified by the interrupt mask are not affected.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on
is the interrupt mask for interrupts to be cleared

briefdescription.para: Get the enabled interrupts of a channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

simplesect.para: The interrupts mask represents pending interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: DMA transfer into memory

briefdescription.para: Interrupt type for setting up callback.

detaileddescription.para: Non-Error Interrupt Type

briefdescription.para: Flag to signal that device is ready to be used.
Set the channel to enable access to higher Frame Buffer Addresses (SG=0)

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

briefdescription.para: Set the channel to disable access higher Frame Buffer Addresses (SG=0)

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the channel to work on

briefdescription.para: Frame/Line Mismatch Error This is a typical DMA Internal Error, which on detection does not require a reset (as opposed to other errors).

detaileddescription.para: So a MSB bit is set to identify it, from other DMA Internal Errors.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be read

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XAxiVdma_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiVdma_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Implementation of the driver API functions for the AXI Video DMA engine.

detaileddescription.para: Look up the hardware settings using device ID.
Contains diagnostic/self-test functions for the XAxiVdma component.
This is a guide on how to move from using the xvdma driver to use xaxivdma driver.
Implementation of interrupt related functions.
Internal API definitions shared by driver files.
Hardware definition file.
Provide a template for user to define their own hardware settings.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 08/16/10 First release 2.00a jz 12/10/10 Added support for direct register access mode, v3 core 2.01a jz 01/19/11 Added ability to re-assign BD addresses rkv 03/28/11 Added support for frame store register. 3.00a srt 08/26/11 Added support for Flush on Frame Sync and dynamic programming of Line Buffer Thresholds and added API XAxiVdma_SetLineBufThreshold. 4.00a srt 11/21/11 - XAxiVdma_CfgInitialize API is modified to use the EffectiveAddr.
Added APIs: XAxiVdma_FsyncSrcSelect() XAxiVdma_GenLockSourceSelect() 4.01a srt 06/13/12 - Added APIs: XAxiVdma_GetDmaChannelErrors() XAxiVdma_ClearDmaChannelErrors() 4.02a srt 09/25/12 - Fixed CR 678734 XAxiVdma_SetFrmStore function changed to remove Reset logic after setting number of frame stores. 4.03a srt 01/18/13 - Updated logic of GenLockSourceSelect() & FsyncSrcSelect() APIs for newer versions of IP (CR: 691052).
Modified CfgInitialize() API to initialize StreamWidth parameters. (CR 691866) 4.04a srt 03/03/13 - Support for *_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_* debug configuration parameters (CR: 703738) 6.1 sk 11/10/15 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress CR# 867425. Changed the prototype of XAxiVdma_CfgInitialize API. 6.6 rsp 07/02/18 Add Vertical flip support. Populate "HasVFlip" from XAxiVdma_Config(CR-989453)

detaileddescription.para: Implementation of the channel related functions. These functions are used internally by the driver, and are declared in xaxivdma_i.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 08/16/10 First release 2.00a jz 12/10/10 Added support for direct register access mode, v3 core 2.01a jz 01/19/11 Added ability to re-assign BD addresses rkv 03/28/11 XAxiVdma_ChannelInit API is changed. 3.02a srt 08/26/11 - XAxiVdma_ChannelErrors API is changed to support for Flush on Frame Sync feature.
Two flags, XST_VDMA_MISMATCH_ERROR & XAXIVDMA_MIS MATCH_ERROR are added to report error status when Flush on Frame Sync feature is enabled. 4.00a srt 11/21/11 - XAxiVdma_ChannelSetBufferAddr API is changed to support 32 Frame Stores.
XAxiVdma_ChannelConfig API is changed to support modified Park Offset Register bits.
Added APIs: XAxiVdma_ChannelHiFrmAddrEnable() XAxiVdma_ChannelHiFrmAddrDisable() 4.01a srt 06/13/12 - Added API: XAxiVdma_ClearChannelErrors() XAxiVdma_ChannelGetEnabledIntr()
XAxiVdma_ChannelErrors API is changed to remove Mismatch error logic.
Removed Error checking logic in the APIs. Provided User APIs to do this. 4.04a srt 03/03/13 - Changes for IPv5.04a: Support for the GenlockRepeat Control bit (Bit 15) (CR: 691391)
Support for *_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_* debug configuration parameters (CR: 703738) 4.05a srt 04/26/13 - Added unalignment checks for Hsize and Stride (CR 710279) 6.6 rsp 07/02/18 - Program vertical flip state for S2MM channel

detaileddescription.para: If using XPS, this file will be automatically generated.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 08/16/10 First release 2.00a jz 12/10/10 Added support for direct register access mode, v3 core 2.01a jz 01/19/11 Added ability to re-assign BD addresses rkv 04/07/11 added new configuration parameter for enabling read of video parameters. 3.00a srt 08/26/11 Added new parameters for Flush on Frame Sync and Line Buffer Thresholds. 4.00a srt 11/21/11 Added new parameters for Genlock Source and Fsync Source Selection. 4.03a srt 01/18/13 Added TDATA_WIDTH parameters (CR: 691866) 4.04a srt 03/03/13 Support for *_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_* debug configuration parameters (CR: 703738) 5.1 sha 07/15/15 Added XPAR_XAXIVDMA_NUM_INSTANCES macro to control config table parameters.

detaileddescription.para: It defines the register interface and Buffer Descriptor (BD) definitions.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 07/30/10 First release 2.00a jz 12/10/10 Added support for direct register access mode, v3 core 2.01a jz 01/19/11 Added ability to re-assign BD addresses rkv 03/28/11 Added support for frame store register. 3.00a srt 08/26/11 - Added support for Line Buffer Threshold Registers.
XAXIVDMA_MISMATCH_ERROR flag is added to support Flush on Frame Sync for Mismatch Errors. 4.00a srt 11/21/11 - XAxiVdma_ChannelSetBufferAddr API is changed to support 32 Frame Stores.
XAxiVdma_ChannelConfig API is changed to support modified Park Offset Register bits.
Added APIs: XAxiVdma_FsyncSrcSelect() XAxiVdma_GenLockSourceSelect() XAxiVdma_ChannelHiFrmAddrEnable() XAxiVdma_ChannelHiFrmAddrDisable() 4.01a srt 06/13/12 - Added new error bit mask XAXIVDMA_SR_ERR_SOF_LATE_MASK
XAXIVDMA_MISMATCH_ERROR flag is deprecated. 4.02a srt 10/11/12 - Changed Error bitmasks to support IP version 5.02a. (CR 679959) 4.04a srt 03/03/13 - Support for the GenlockRepeat Control bit (Bit 15) added in the new version of IP v5.04 (CR: 691391) 4.06a srt 04/09/13 - Added support for the newly added S2MM_DMA_IRQ_MASK register (CR 734741) 6.6 rsp 07/02/18 - Add offset and mask for Vertical flip register

preformatted.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 08/18/10 First release 2.00a jz 12/10/10 Added support for direct register access mode, v3 core 2.01a jz 01/19/11 Added ability to re-assign BD addresses Replaced include xenv.h with string.h in xaxivdma_i.h file. rkv 03/28/11 Added support for frame store register. 3.00a srt 08/26/11 Added support for Flush on Frame Sync and dynamic programming of Line Buffer Thresholds. 4.00a srt 11/21/11 Added support for 32 Frame Stores and modified bit mask of Park Offset Register. Added support for GenLock & Fsync Source Selection. 4.03a srt 01/18/13 - Added StreamWidth parameter to XAxiVdma_Channel structure (CR 691866). 4.04a srt 03/03/13 Support for the GenlockRepeat Control bit (Bit 15) added in the new version of IP v5.04 (CR: 691391) Support for *_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_* debug configuration parameters (CR: 703738) 6.6 rsp 07/02/18 Add vertical flip states in config structures
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 08/16/10 First release 2.00a jz 12/10/10 Added support for direct register access mode, v3 core 2.01a jz 01/19/11 Added ability to re-assign BD addresses 4.01a srt 06/13/12 Modified the logic of Error handling in interrupt handlers. 4.06a srt 04/09/13 Added API XAxiVdma_MaskS2MMErrIntr which will mask the S2MM interrupt for the error mask provided. (CR 734741) 6.3 ms 02/20/17 Fixed compilation error. CR-969129.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes ----- ---- -------- ------------------------------------------------------- 1.00a jz 08/16/10 First release 2.00a jz 12/10/10 Added support for direct register access mode, v3 core 2.01a jz 01/19/11 Added ability to re-assign BD addresses rkv 03/28/11 Added support for frame store register. 3.00a srt 08/26/11 - Added support for Flush on Frame Sync and dynamic programming of Line Buffer Thresholds.
XAxiVdma_ChannelErrors API is changed to support for Flush on Frame Sync feature.
Two flags, XST_VDMA_MISMATCH_ERROR & XAXIVDMA_MIS MATCH_ERROR are added to report error status when Flush on Frame Sync feature is enabled. 4.00a srt 11/21/11 - XAxiVdma_ChannelSetBufferAddr API is changed to support 32 Frame Stores.
XAxiVdma_ChannelConfig API is changed to support modified Park Offset Register bits.
Added APIs: XAxiVdma_FsyncSrcSelect() XAxiVdma_GenLockSourceSelect()
Modified structures XAxiVdma_Config and XAxiVdma to include new parameters. 4.01a srt 06/13/12 - Added APIs: XAxiVdma_GetDmaChannelErrors() XAxiVdma_ClearDmaChannelErrors() XAxiVdma_ClearChannelErrors()
XAxiVdma_ChannelErrors API is changed to remove Mismatch error logic.
Removed Error checking logic in the channel APIs. Provided User APIs to do this.
Added new error bit mask XAXIVDMA_SR_ERR_SOF_LATE_MASK
XAXIVDMA_MISMATCH_ERROR flag is deprecated.
Modified the logic of Error handling in interrupt handlers.

The API for xaxivdma driver is similar to xvdma driver. However, the prefix for functions and structures is now XAxiVdma_. Due to hardware changes, for AXI_VDMA core in 12.3, we have changed some API functions, removed some API functions and added some new API functions.

From 13.1 release, the hardware, v3.00a core supports direct register access mode where transfer is set through direct register writes. Scatter gather is no longer there if the direct register access mode is selected. This change does not impact the driver API.
For 13.2 release, the driver supports re-assigning BD addresses. This comes from the user that it is desirable to change the BD addresses to a memory region that SG hardware can access.

For 13.4 release, the hardware, v5.00a core has added new features to select Internal GenLock and Frame Sync Sources in the control register. Due to hardware changes, we have added new API functions for the two new features.
For 14.2 release, the hardware, v5.02a core has modified the handling of VDMA errors. To support this, we have changed some APIs and added some new APIs.

preformatted.bold: API That Only Have Prefix Changes

preformatted.preformatted: Note that API includes data structures and functions. For API functions that use VDMA data structures or their pointers as arguments, the structures need to use the new prefix as well.

The following are the data structures that have prefix changed: xvdma API structures | xaxivdma API structures
---------------------------------|--------------------------- XVDma | XAxiVdma XVDma_Config | XAxiVdma_Config XVDma_DmaSetup | XAxiVdma_DmaSetup XVDmaFrameCounter | XAxiVdma_FrameCounter

The following is the list of API functions that only have the prefix changes: xvdma API functions | xaxivdma API functions
---------------------------------|--------------------------- XVDma_CfgInitialize | XAxiVdma_CfgInitialize XVDma_StartWriteFrame | XAxiVdma_StartWriteFrame XVDma_StartReadFrame | XAxiVdma_StartReadFrame XVDma_SetFrameCounter | XAxiVdma_SetFrameCounter XVDma_GetFrameCounter | XAxiVdma_GetFrameCounter XVDma_LookupConfig | XAxiVdma_LookupConfig

preformatted.bold: API Functions That Have Been Changed

preformatted.preformatted:
PLB VDMA only has one channel, while the AXI VDMA has two channels. This change has caused some of the API functions to take an extra argument to differentiate the channels. The following functions now take an extra argument (u16 Direction), the new argument is the last in the argument list: xvdma API functions | xaxivdma API functions
---------------------------------|--------------------------- XVDma_Reset | XAxiVdma_Reset XVDma_IsBusy | XAxiVdma_IsBusy XVDma_CurrFrameStore | XAxiVdma_CurrFrameStore XVDma_IntrEnable | XAxiVdma_IntrEnable XVDma_IntrDisable | XAxiVdma_IntrDisable XVDma_IntrGetPending | XAxiVdma_IntrGetPending XVDma_IntrClear | XAxiVdma_IntrClear XVDma_SetCallBack | XAxiVdma_SetCallBack
Note that the xvdma API functions that already have Direction as the argument are kept as they are, which are not necessarily having Direction as the last argument.

Some API functions have changed return type from void to int. This is to make the driver more intelligent and signal the failure early on to the user. The following functions have change return type from void to int: xvdma API functions | xaxivdma API functions
---------------------------------|--------------------------- void XVDma_DmaConfig | int XAxiVdma_DmaConfig void XVDma_DmaSetBufferAddr | int XAxiVdma_DmaSetBufferAddr void XVDma_DmaStart | int XAxiVdma_DmaStart
New core v5.02a has added new features, to support them these APIs are added

int XAxiVdma_GetDmaChannelErrors(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u16 Direction) int XAxiVdma_ClearDmaChannelErrors(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u16 Direction, u32 ErrorMask) void XAxiVdma_ClearChannelErrors(XAxiVdma_Channel *Channel, u32 ErrorMask)
New core v5.00a has added new features, to support them these APIs are added
int XAxiVdma_FsyncSrcSelect(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u32 Source, u16 Direction) int XAxiVdma_GenLockSourceSelect(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u32 Source, u16 Direction)
Due to addition of frame store register in hardware these functions are added
int XAxiVdma_SetFrmStore(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u8 FrmStoreNum, u16 Direction) void XAxiVdma_GetFrmStore(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u8 *FrmStoreNum, u16 Direction)
Due to the fact that AXI VDMA has two channels, and each channel has its own interrupt ID, we removed the single interrupt handler API from xvdma driver, and added two interrupt handlers:
. void XAxiVdma_ReadIntrHandler(void *Ref) . void XAxiVdma_WriteIntrHandler(void *Ref)
Based on customers feedback on xvdma, we added XAxiVdma_Stop to stop one channel:
. void XAxiVdma_Stop(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u16 Direction)
We need a function to check whether reset is done:
. int XAxiVdma_ResetNotDone(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u16 Direction)
While doing the development, it shows the need for getting the hardware status to provide as much information as possible for user's development.
. u32 XAxiVdma_GetStatus(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u16 Direction)

Because the hardware limitations, parking mode cannot be the initial mode to start the DMA engine. Entering and exiting parking mode have to be done after the hardware is running. Therefore, we add two API functions to enter and exit park mode.
Note that configuring the hardware to be in park mode using XAxiVdma_DmaConfig() is fine. The advantage of using _StartParking() and _StopParking() is the simplicity.
. void XAxiVdma_StartFrmCntEnable(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u16 Direction);
This function is used to enable frame count enable. It is much simpler to enable frame count enable this way than using the full configuration structure.
. int XAxiVdma_SetBdAddrs(XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u32 BdAddrPhys, u32 BdAddrVirt, int NumBds, u16 Direction);

This function provides the ability for the user application to re-assign the BD addresses to wherever in the memory it wants. This is to aid the situation that SG engine may have limited access to certain region of the memory, comparing to the processor's ability. The default BD addresses are inside the region that the processor can access.

preformatted.bold: API Functions That Have Been Removed

preformatted.preformatted: Due to hardware design change, some of the API functions are no longer needed:

. XVDma_CmdInerfaceDisable() . XVDma_CmdInerfaceEnable() . XVDma_FullDuplexDisable() . XVDma_FullDuplexEnable() . XVDma_HoriCropDisable() . XVDma_HoriCropEnable() . XVDma_IntrEnableGlobal() . XVDma_IntrDisableGlobal() . XVDma_IntrHandler()
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
5.1 adk 29/01/15 First release
The hardware setting is inside the configuration table in xaxivdma_g.c, generated automatically by XPS or manually by user.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 08/16/10 First release 2.00a jz 12/10/10 Added support for direct register access mode, v3 core 2.01a jz 01/19/11 Added ability to re-assign BD addresses 5.1 sha 07/15/15 Defined macro XPAR_XAXIVDMA_NUM_INSTANCES if not defined in xparameters.h 6.4 ms 08/07/17 Fixed compilation warnings.
